FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional bank M&A kicks off with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while JPMorgan and Goldman post record revenues on AI-fueled trading and banking; regulatory scrutiny tightens on immigrant lending.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaii's largest bank returning to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs posted record revenues, with AI-fueled trading and investment banking capturing outsized deal flow on Wall Street.
Regulators issued guidance requiring banks to identify and control lending risks tied to unauthorized workers—compliance signal that could tighten credit standards for immigrant borrowers.
JPMorgan's Dimon clarified Mary Erdoes retirement as 'natural change' after board appointed Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh as co-presidents.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Mega-deal season in wealth management: CalPERS buys Russell Investments, LPL poaches $2.4B team from U.S. Bank, and Vanguard finally courts RIAs after years of cold shoulder.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments ($580B pension system gets multi-asset manager; Russell ditches debt and goes non-profit, following Vanguard's playbook).
LPL Financial recruits $2.4B HNW team from U.S. Bank via Quotient OSJ, signaling aggressive poaching in the high-net-worth advisory space.
Creative Planning ($710B RIA) buys Lovell Insurance Group to bundle commercial insurance into upper-middle-market advisory offering.
Vanguard hires Beverly Goodman to build RIA relationships—a significant 180 after decades of institutional skepticism toward third-party advisors and their fees.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: 5 insurance stories: structural shift toward hybrid capital, M&A in underwriting tech, cyber expansion, UK captive market opening, and regulatory risk in vaping.

bullets.text: Cedents increasingly demanding reinsurers with dual access to traditional + alternative capital—Guy Carpenter's renewal report signals structural market shift in buyer preferences.
Duck Creek acquires Send Technology to build agentic underwriting platform—consolidation trend around AI-driven workflows in core insurance ops.
Coalition launches $25M cyber product for enterprises following Allianz partnership expansion—MGAs pushing upmarket on capacity and coverages.
UK government advancing captive insurance regulation to compete with US/EU jurisdictions—removes structural disadvantage driving UK entities offshore.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Major fintech consolidation bid amid AI investment cautionary notes—Stripe/Advent eye PayPal at $53B while BIS warns of AI bubble risk.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent jointly bid >$53B for PayPal, potentially reshaping fintech's competitive landscape through major consolidation.
BIS flags $1 trillion AI investment boom as bubble risk with potential systemic spillovers into global finance and real economy.
Emirates NBD goes live on Partior's tokenized settlement network for real-time USD cross-border payments; blockchain infrastructure gaining institutional traction.
Flex, an AI-native private banking platform, raised $70M Series B despite broader warnings about AI valuations and sustainability.

PE & Deals.lede: GP financing innovation and secondary funds gain traction, but mid-market PE shows contraction signals amid evolving LP strategies.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closes $6.2bn GP financing using 'self-liquidating' strategy—a Keystone structure that recycles capital across fund cycles without LP capital calls, setting a new precedent for GP-to-LP financing.

Carlyle AlpInvest oversubscribes second single-asset secondary fund at $1.7bn (+70% vs. $1bn target), validating LP appetite for concentrated, thematic secondary vehicles with transparent performance.
Temasek-backed Vertex scales Growth Fund III to $250M—down 40%+ from prior fund—signaling mid-market financial services PE contraction despite broader institutional push into private markets.
Blackstone's new EMEA wealth head targets retail-eligible offerings across fragmented European markets as $177trn-controlled institutional capital deepens private allocations.

Markets & Macro.lede: Stripe-Advent's $53bn PayPal bid dominates, but banking earnings may have peaked as geopolitical risks and credit stress mount.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent Capital jointly bid $53bn for PayPal in major fintech consolidation play; shares up 19% pre-market.
U.S. banks posted record Q2 earnings on IB/trading strength, but geopolitical tension, rate-cut expectations, and CRE exposure signal cycle peak.
Billionaire Drahi's Altice International faces €2bn debt default accusations over covenant-violating asset transfers—high-yield bond stress test.
Oil traders warn strategic reserves near depletion as Strait of Hormuz closes again; supply shock cushion is evaporating.

Newsletters.lede: Wall Street banks post record revenues fueled by trading and M&A, while the Fed signals hawkish inflation stance despite cooling June CPI.

bullets.text: Record Wall Street revenues driven by Iran-related trading volatility and elevated M&A activity; investment banking units saw outsized gains.
Fed's Kevin Warsh signals hard line on inflation to Congress as June CPI sits at 3.5%—cooling from May but still above the 2% target.
iShares SGOV ultra-short bond ETF approaching $100B in assets, poised to become first in its class to hit milestone amid elevated rates.
Vanguard and Envestnet partner to minimize tax friction in model portfolio conversions, addressing RIA workflow friction point.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four stories spanning misaligned incentives in sports, AI infrastructure financing, legacy tech constraints, and OpenAI's platform shift.

bullets.text: Jonathan Ross secured a $20B NVIDIA partnership for Groq via unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) after near-bankruptcy—a masterclass in startup capital creativity during AI infrastructure buildout.
IBM's mainframe cash cow is now a strategic anchor: the legacy franchise generates too much profit to cannibalize with AI products, explaining earnings miss and market concerns.
OpenAI rebranded Codex into ChatGPT, signaling a pivot from conversational chat toward a broader productivity super app—watch for competitive implications across enterprise software.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A and fintech consolidation dominate the day—First Hawaiian buys TriCo for $2B while Stripe and Advent jointly bid $53B for PayPal.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquires California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end. Marks the Hawaii lender's return to mainland U.S. presence after BNP Paribas exit.
Stripe and Advent Capital Partners jointly bid $53B for PayPal; shares surged 19% pre-market. Deal consolidates fintech's two largest payments players under PE backing.
CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for an undisclosed price, allowing the asset manager to shed debt and adopt non-profit governance on the Vanguard model.
